=== BURE Regime

[[bure_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{bure_regime}::
Ambient regime for caller-supplied behavioral overrides to BUK primitives.
Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURE variables are set directly in the caller's environment.
Kindled and validated when buc_command.sh is sourced.

[[bure_countdown]]
//axvr_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_countdown}::
Countdown behavior override.
When set to `skip`, buc_countdown bypasses interactive countdown and tty output.
When unset, countdown proceeds normally with interactive tty cancel window.
Any other value fails validation.

[[bure_verbose]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{bure_verbose}::
Verbosity level controlling diagnostic output.
0=silent, 1=show messages, 2=enable bash trace.

[[bure_color]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{bure_color}::
Color output policy.
Input as auto/0/1; resolved to 0 or 1 during dispatch.
Respects NO_COLOR environment convention.

==== Tweak Mechanism

The tweak mechanism provides a single caller-supplied name/value override that flows through invocation without BUK interpreting its semantics.
BUK enrolls, validates, and propagates the pair; consuming kits (e.g., RBK) check the name and act on the value.

Constraint: one tweak per invocation.
The caller sets at most one
{bure_tweak_name}/{bure_tweak_value}
pair in the environment before invoking a tabtarget.
The pair is ambient — it propagates through `zbuto_invoke` like all BURE variables, without requiring re-kindle in the invoked command.

[[bure_tweak_name]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_tweak_name}::
Consumer-interpreted tweak identifier.
The name selects which behavioral override the consuming kit applies.
BUK validates length (0–64 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
+
Example: `graft_image` — tells RBK to substitute the graft source image.

[[bure_tweak_value]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_tweak_value}::
Consumer-interpreted tweak payload.
The value is opaque to BUK; its format and interpretation are defined by whatever kit checks
{bure_tweak_name}.
BUK validates length (0–256 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
+
Either variable may be set independently — BUK does not enforce pairing.
Consumer kits that require both must enforce that themselves.

==== Exchange Labels

[[bure_label]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_xname axd_optional
{bure_label}::
Correlation label for cross-project exchange.
When set, propagated verbatim into
{burx_label}
during dispatch.
When unset, BUD writes empty string.
Maximum 120 characters, xname format.
+
BUD writes whatever
{bure_label}
contains without interpreting the semantics.

=== BURX Regime

[[burx_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burx_prefix}::
Bash Utility Exchange Regime

[[burx_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{burx_regime}::
Exchange regime for cross-project dispatch state.
Written by bud_dispatch.sh to both
{burd_output_dir}
and
{burd_temp_dir}
as `burx.env` via
{buf_write_fact_single}.
Unlike single-project regimes,
{burx_regime}
is designed to be read across a remote project boundary.
+
Variables appear in two phases:
initial fields are written at dispatch start;
terminal fields are appended at dispatch completion.
Absence of
{burx_exit_status}
indicates the dispatch is still running.

[[burx_pid]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burx_pid}::
Process ID of the dispatched command.

[[burx_began_at]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burx_began_at}::
Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch started.
Format: `YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN`.

[[burx_tabtarget]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burx_tabtarget}::
The tabtarget that was dispatched.
Corresponds to
{burd_target}.

[[burx_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burx_temp_dir}::
Absolute path to the dispatch temporary directory.
Corresponds to
{burd_temp_dir}.

[[burx_transcript]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burx_transcript}::
Absolute path to the transcript file.
Corresponds to
{burd_transcript}.

[[burx_log_hist]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burx_log_hist}::
Absolute path to the historical log file.
Corresponds to
{burd_log_hist}.

[[burx_label]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burx_label}::
Correlation label from
{bure_label}.
Always present — empty string when
{bure_label}
is not set.
Format and interpretation are opaque to BUD.

[[burx_exit_status]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer axd_optional
{burx_exit_status}::
Unix exit code of the dispatched command.
Absence indicates the dispatch is still running.

[[burx_ended_at]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burx_ended_at}::
Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch completed.
Same format as
{burx_began_at}.
Present only when
{burx_exit_status}
is present.

== Remote Node Access

Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.

=== Privilege Tiers

The {burn_tier} field of every {burn_regime} profile classifies the profile
as either `privileged` or `workload`. Tabtargets refuse aliases of the wrong
tier at entry.

==== Privileged Tier

Privileged profiles authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or equivalent
privileges. Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user
provisioning, node-establishment ceremonies.

Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.

==== Workload Tier

Workload profiles authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
privileged authority. Used for normal remote work: running commands,
file operations, interactive shells.

Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
station users. Lifecycle is short:
{busn_conscript}
when needed,
{busn_discharge}
when done.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.

==== Lifecycle

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget

List all {burn_regime} profiles in the local station subtree.

Render a single {burn_regime} profile (alias passed as positional argument).

[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget

Validate a single {burn_regime} profile (alias passed as positional argument).

{bust_conscript_cygwin}::
`buw-nwcc` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Cygwin shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_wsl}::
`buw-nwcw` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a WSL shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_powershell]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_powershell}::
`buw-nwcp` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a PowerShell shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_localhost]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_localhost}::
`buw-nwcx` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a localhost shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_discharge]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_discharge}::
`buw-nwd` —
{busn_discharge} a workload user.
Deletes the remote OS user via the workload profile's privileged
authority and removes the local profile.

[[bust_inventory]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_inventory}::
`buw-nwi` —
{busn_inventory} workload users present on the node, queried via the
privileged BURN.

==== Workload (operational)

[[bust_knock]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_knock}::
`buw-nwk` —
Probe workload responsiveness via passwordless SSH.

[[bust_remote_run]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_remote_run}::
`buw-nwr` —
Run the contents of a command file on the remote node; capture output to
{burd_output_dir}.

[[bust_interactive_session]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_interactive_session}::
`buw-nws` —
Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.

==== Handbook

[[bust_handbook_node_top]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_node_top}::
`buw-hn0` —
Top-level handbook landing for node access procedures.

=== Privileges

The {burs_user} owns its profile subtree at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/`
exclusively — operations write only under the current station user's tree.

A {burn_regime} profile describes a connection identity (host, user, shell,
alias, key, tier) — not project state, not work-directory. Project context
is supplied by the consumer at invocation time.

Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
the Administrators group).

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Establish privileged BURN trust on a node by writing the privileged
{burn_regime} profile and running the platform-specific ceremony to gain
admin access. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-npg{platform}`. First-time
node setup; idempotent on re-run.

[[busn_conscript]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_conscript}::
Create an ephemeral workload user on a node and provision a workload
{burn_regime} profile locally. Requires a privileged BURN as authority for
the remote-user creation. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-nwc{platform}`,
where the platform letter selects the workload shell — independent of the
privileged BURN's shell choice.

[[busn_discharge]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_discharge}::
Remove a workload user from a node and delete the local workload
{burn_regime} profile. The workload profile encodes its privileged
authority; {busn_discharge} uses that authority to delete the remote OS
user. Tabtarget: `buw-nwd`.

[[busn_inventory]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_inventory}::
List workload users currently provisioned on a node, queried via a
privileged BURN. Scope is the node's full workload-user population — not
just the current station user's history. Tabtarget: `buw-nwi`.

=== Input Artifacts

[[busi_scribed_command_file]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busi_scribed_command_file}::
A file authored by the operator containing commands to be executed
on the remote node by {bust_remote_run}.
Path passed as the second positional argument; contents are streamed
to the remote shell at dispatch time.

include::BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc[]

include::BUSNM-NodeMac.adoc[]

include::BUSTLL-NodeRegimeList.adoc[]

include::BUSTLR-NodeRegimeRender.adoc[]

include::BUSTLV-NodeRegimeValidate.adoc[]

include::BUSTGC-GarrisonCygwin.adoc[]

include::BUSTGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc[]

include::BUSTGP-GarrisonPowershell.adoc[]

include::BUSTCC-ConscriptCygwin.adoc[]

include::BUSTCW-ConscriptWsl.adoc[]

include::BUSTCP-ConscriptPowershell.adoc[]

include::BUSTWD-Discharge.adoc[]

include::BUSTWI-Inventory.adoc[]

include::BUSTOK-Knock.adoc[]

include::BUSTOE-RemoteRun.adoc[]

include::BUSTOS-InteractiveSession.adoc[]

include::BUSTHT-HandbookNodeTop.adoc[]

1	=== Windows Node Access
2	
3	Platform-specific ceremony and contracts for Windows node access.
4	
5	==== Status
6	
7	Stub.
8	Elaborate content to be drafted by subsequent paces — Windows is the
9	canonical worked example for the Remote Node Access ceremony, requiring
10	detailed coverage of OpenSSH server provisioning, the
11	`administrators_authorized_keys` special case, ACL fixes via `icacls`,
12	and the temp-password bootstrap handshake.
13	
14	==== Privilege Contract
15	
16	The {burn_user} of a Windows privileged BURN must be a member of the local
17	Administrators group.
18	Rationale: Windows OpenSSH consults `C:\ProgramData\ssh\administrators_authorized_keys`
19	for users in the Administrators group, ignoring the per-user
20	`~/.ssh/authorized_keys` for those users.
21	A non-admin path would require parallel implementation with no return for
22	the supported use cases.
23	
24	Asserted at every ceremony participant before any modification.
25	
26	==== Platform Letters
27	
28	* `c` — Cygwin (`buw-npgc`, `buw-nwcc`)
29	* `w` — WSL (`buw-npgw`, `buw-nwcw`)
30	* `p` — PowerShell (`buw-npgp`, `buw-nwcp`)
31	
32	==== Out of Band
33	
34	The console phase of the Windows ceremony is performed at the Windows
35	physical keyboard (or RDP) by the operator and is described in the BUK
36	handbook surface, not in this spec.
37

file.content:
=== Windows Node Access

Platform-specific ceremony and contracts for Windows node access.

==== Status

Stub.
Elaborate content to be drafted by subsequent paces — Windows is the
canonical worked example for the Remote Node Access ceremony, requiring
detailed coverage of OpenSSH server provisioning, the
`administrators_authorized_keys` special case, ACL fixes via `icacls`,
and the temp-password bootstrap handshake.

==== Privilege Contract

The {burn_user} of a Windows privileged BURN must be a member of the local
Administrators group.
Rationale: Windows OpenSSH consults `C:\ProgramData\ssh\administrators_authorized_keys`
for users in the Administrators group, ignoring the per-user
`~/.ssh/authorized_keys` for those users.
A non-admin path would require parallel implementation with no return for
the supported use cases.

Asserted at every ceremony participant before any modification.

==== Platform Letters

* `c` — Cygwin (`buw-npgc`, `buw-nwcc`)
* `w` — WSL (`buw-npgw`, `buw-nwcw`)
* `p` — PowerShell (`buw-npgp`, `buw-nwcp`)

==== Out of Band

The console phase of the Windows ceremony is performed at the Windows
physical keyboard (or RDP) by the operator and is described in the BUK
handbook surface, not in this spec.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_garrison_cygwin}
3	
4	NOTE: Establishes privileged BURN trust against a Cygwin node by writing
5	the privileged {burn_regime} profile and driving the Cygwin-specific
6	{busn_garrison} ceremony.
7	Idempotent on re-run.
8	
9	//axhopt_typed_parameter
10	{burn_alias}
11	
12	//axhoq_precondition
13	The remote Windows account named in {burn_user} is a member of the
14	local Administrators group on the target node.
15	
16	//axhoq_precondition
17	OpenSSH server is reachable on {burn_host} from the calling station,
18	and the operator can complete the console-side handshake at the
19	Windows keyboard (or RDP).
20	
21	//axhos_step
22	. *Write profile*: persist the privileged {burn_regime} profile to the
23	station subtree with `{burn_tier} = privileged`
24	
25	//axhos_step
26	. *Console handshake*: drive the Cygwin-specific console ceremony to
27	install the station pubkey into
28	`C:\ProgramData\ssh\administrators_authorized_keys`
29	
30	//axhos_step
31	. *Probe trust*: confirm passwordless SSH to the privileged account
32	succeeds via {bust_knock}
33	
34	//axhog_guarantee
35	The privileged {burn_regime} profile is persisted locally and the
36	station's {burn_ssh_pubkey} is trusted on the remote Cygwin node.
37	
38	//axhoc_completion
39	Exit 0 when the trust handshake completes and the verification probe
40	succeeds.
41	Non-zero on profile-write failure, console-handshake refusal, or final
42	SSH probe failure.
43

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_garrison_wsl}
3	
4	NOTE: Establishes privileged BURN trust against a WSL node by writing
5	the privileged {burn_regime} profile and driving the WSL-specific
6	{busn_garrison} ceremony.
7	Idempotent on re-run.
8	
9	//axhopt_typed_parameter
10	{burn_alias}
11	
12	//axhoq_precondition
13	The remote Windows account hosting the WSL distribution is a member of
14	the local Administrators group, and the WSL distribution named in
15	{burn_command} is registered for that account on the target node.
16	
17	//axhoq_precondition
18	OpenSSH server is reachable on {burn_host} from the calling station,
19	and the operator can complete the console-side handshake at the
20	Windows keyboard (or RDP).
21	
22	//axhos_step
23	. *Write profile*: persist the privileged {burn_regime} profile to the
24	station subtree with `{burn_tier} = privileged` and the WSL launcher
25	recorded in {burn_command}
26	
27	//axhos_step
28	. *Console handshake*: drive the WSL-specific console ceremony — bash
29	owns control flow and PowerShell is the syscall layer — to install the
30	station pubkey into
31	`C:\ProgramData\ssh\administrators_authorized_keys`
32	
33	//axhos_step
34	. *Probe trust*: confirm passwordless SSH to the privileged account
35	succeeds via {bust_knock}
36	
37	//axhog_guarantee
38	The privileged {burn_regime} profile is persisted locally and the
39	station's {burn_ssh_pubkey} is trusted on the remote WSL node.
40	
41	//axhoc_completion
42	Exit 0 when the trust handshake completes and the verification probe
43	succeeds.
44	Non-zero on profile-write failure, WSL launcher unavailability,
45	console-handshake refusal, or final SSH probe failure.
46

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_garrison_powershell}
3	
4	NOTE: Establishes privileged BURN trust against a Windows node using
5	PowerShell as the remote shell, by writing the privileged
6	{burn_regime} profile and driving the PowerShell-specific
7	{busn_garrison} ceremony.
8	Idempotent on re-run.
9	
10	//axhopt_typed_parameter
11	{burn_alias}
12	
13	//axhoq_precondition
14	The remote Windows account named in {burn_user} is a member of the
15	local Administrators group on the target node.
16	
17	//axhoq_precondition
18	OpenSSH server is reachable on {burn_host} from the calling station,
19	the OpenSSH default shell is set (or settable) to `powershell.exe`,
20	and the operator can complete the console-side handshake at the
21	Windows keyboard (or RDP).
22	
23	//axhos_step
24	. *Write profile*: persist the privileged {burn_regime} profile to the
25	station subtree with `{burn_tier} = privileged` and {burn_command}
26	naming the PowerShell launcher
27	
28	//axhos_step
29	. *Console handshake*: drive the PowerShell-specific console ceremony
30	to install the station pubkey into
31	`C:\ProgramData\ssh\administrators_authorized_keys`
32	
33	//axhos_step
34	. *Probe trust*: confirm passwordless SSH to the privileged account
35	succeeds via {bust_knock}
36	
37	//axhog_guarantee
38	The privileged {burn_regime} profile is persisted locally and the
39	station's {burn_ssh_pubkey} is trusted on the remote Windows node
40	under the PowerShell shell choice.
41	
42	//axhoc_completion
43	Exit 0 when the trust handshake completes and the verification probe
44	succeeds.
45	Non-zero on profile-write failure, console-handshake refusal, or final
46	SSH probe failure.
47

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUK Windows Commands
20	#
21	# Windows-specific automation commands that alter system configuration.
22	# These are commands (not handbooks) — they modify state on the target machine.
23	# Consumes buhw tinder constants for Windows paths and network constants.
24	
25	set -euo pipefail
26	
27	# Multiple inclusion detection
28	test -z "${ZBUWC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buwc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
29	ZBUWC_SOURCED=1
30	
31	######################################################################
32	# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel
33	
34	zbuwc_kindle() {
35	  test -z "${ZBUWC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buwc already kindled"
36	  zbuhw_sentinel
37	  readonly ZBUWC_KINDLED=1
38	}
39	
40	zbuwc_sentinel() {
41	  test "${ZBUWC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buwc not kindled - call zbuwc_kindle first"
42	}
43	
44	######################################################################
45	# External Functions (buwc_*)
46	
47	buwc_install_key() {
48	  zbuwc_sentinel
49	
50	  buc_doc_brief "Install BURN profile SSH key into Windows administrators authorized_keys (idempotent)"
51	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
52	
53	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
54	
55	  command -v powershell.exe >/dev/null 2>&1 \
56	    || buc_die "powershell.exe not found — this command must run from WSL"
57	
58	  zburn_build_key_line
59	
60	  # Read existing file, remove old marker line, append new line — all via PowerShell
61	  local z_existing=""
62	  if z_existing=$(powershell.exe -Command "Get-Content -Path '${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" 2>/dev/null); then
63	    z_existing=$(printf '%s\n' "${z_existing}" | grep -v "${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}" || true)
64	  fi
65	
66	  {
67	    test -z "${z_existing}" || printf '%s\n' "${z_existing}"
68	    printf '%s\n' "${ZBURN_KEY_LINE}"
69	  } | powershell.exe -Command "\$input | Set-Content -Path '${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}' -Force" \
70	    || buc_die "Failed to write authorized_keys (elevated terminal required)"
71	
72	  # Windows admin authorized_keys requires specific ACL
73	  powershell.exe -Command "icacls '${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}' /inheritance:r /grant 'Administrators:F' /grant 'SYSTEM:F'" \
74	    || buc_die "Failed to set authorized_keys permissions"
75	
76	  buc_success "Key installed for ${BURN_ALIAS} in ${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}"
77	}
78	
79	buwc_bootstrap_sshd() {
80	  zbuwc_sentinel
81	
82	  buc_doc_brief "Provision OpenSSH server on Windows via PowerShell from WSL"
83	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
84	
85	  buc_require \
86	    "This will OVERWRITE sshd_config, restart sshd service, and create firewall rules on the Windows host." \
87	    "OVERWRITE"
88	
89	  command -v powershell.exe >/dev/null 2>&1 \
90	    || buc_die "powershell.exe not found — this command must run from WSL"
91	
92	  buc_step "Bootstrap: OpenSSH Server on Windows"
93	
94	  # Step 1: Probe sshd
95	  buc_step "Probing OpenSSH Server..."
96	  local z_status=""
97	  if z_status=$(powershell.exe -Command 'Get-Service sshd -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Status' 2>&1); then
98	    z_status=$(printf '%s' "${z_status}" | tr -d '\r\n')
99	    buc_step "OpenSSH Server status: ${z_status}"
100	  else
101	    buc_step "OpenSSH Server not installed — installing..."
102	    powershell.exe -Command 'Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0' \
103	      || buc_die "Failed to install OpenSSH Server (elevated terminal required)"
104	    buc_step "OpenSSH Server installed"
105	  fi
106	
107	  # Step 2: Write sshd_config from template
108	  buc_step "Writing sshd_config..."
109	  {
110	    echo "# Generated by buw-wcb — BURN bootstrap"
111	    echo "Port ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
112	    echo "PasswordAuthentication no"
113	    echo "PubkeyAuthentication yes"
114	    echo "PermitEmptyPasswords no"
115	    echo "AuthorizedKeysFile .ssh/authorized_keys"
116	    echo "Subsystem sftp sftp-server.exe"
117	    echo "Match Group administrators"
118	    echo "  AuthorizedKeysFile __PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys"
119	  } | powershell.exe -Command "\$input | Set-Content -Path '${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG}' -Force" \
120	    || buc_die "Failed to write sshd_config (elevated terminal required)"
121	  buc_step "sshd_config written"
122	
123	  # Validate sshd_config
124	  powershell.exe -Command "sshd -t -f '${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG}'" \
125	    || buc_die "sshd_config validation failed — check config syntax"
126	  buc_step "sshd_config validated"
127	
128	  # Step 3: Configure and start sshd service
129	  buc_step "Configuring sshd service..."
130	  powershell.exe -Command 'Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic' \
131	    || buc_die "Failed to configure sshd service"
132	  powershell.exe -Command 'Restart-Service sshd' \
133	    || buc_die "Failed to restart sshd service"
134	  buc_step "sshd service configured and started"
135	
136	  # Step 4: Firewall rule (idempotent)
137	  buc_step "Checking firewall rule..."
138	  if powershell.exe -Command "Get-NetFirewallRule -Name '${ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME}' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
139	    buc_step "Firewall rule already exists"
140	  else
141	    powershell.exe -Command "New-NetFirewallRule -Name '${ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME}' -DisplayName '${ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME}' -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}" \
142	      || buc_die "Failed to create firewall rule"
143	    buc_step "Firewall rule created"
144	  fi
145	
146	  # Step 5: Loopback verify
147	  buc_step "Verifying loopback SSH..."
148	  local z_whoami=""
149	  if z_whoami=$(ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no localhost whoami 2>&1); then
150	    z_whoami=$(printf '%s' "${z_whoami}" | tr -d '\r\n')
151	    buc_success "Bootstrap complete — loopback SSH: ${z_whoami}"
152	  else
153	    buc_warn "Loopback verify deferred (keys may need setup from client machine)"
154	    buc_step "Bootstrap structurally complete"
155	  fi
156	}
157	
158	# eof
159

# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUK Windows Commands
#
# Windows-specific automation commands that alter system configuration.
# These are commands (not handbooks) — they modify state on the target machine.
# Consumes buhw tinder constants for Windows paths and network constants.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBUWC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buwc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUWC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

zbuwc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUWC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buwc already kindled"
  zbuhw_sentinel
  readonly ZBUWC_KINDLED=1
}

zbuwc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUWC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buwc not kindled - call zbuwc_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (buwc_*)

buwc_install_key() {
  zbuwc_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Install BURN profile SSH key into Windows administrators authorized_keys (idempotent)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"

  command -v powershell.exe >/dev/null 2>&1 \
    || buc_die "powershell.exe not found — this command must run from WSL"

  zburn_build_key_line

  # Read existing file, remove old marker line, append new line — all via PowerShell
  local z_existing=""
  if z_existing=$(powershell.exe -Command "Get-Content -Path '${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" 2>/dev/null); then
    z_existing=$(printf '%s\n' "${z_existing}" | grep -v "${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}" || true)
  fi

  {
    test -z "${z_existing}" || printf '%s\n' "${z_existing}"
    printf '%s\n' "${ZBURN_KEY_LINE}"
  } | powershell.exe -Command "\$input | Set-Content -Path '${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}' -Force" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write authorized_keys (elevated terminal required)"

  # Windows admin authorized_keys requires specific ACL
  powershell.exe -Command "icacls '${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}' /inheritance:r /grant 'Administrators:F' /grant 'SYSTEM:F'" \
    || buc_die "Failed to set authorized_keys permissions"

  buc_success "Key installed for ${BURN_ALIAS} in ${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}"
}

buwc_bootstrap_sshd() {
  zbuwc_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Provision OpenSSH server on Windows via PowerShell from WSL"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_require \
    "This will OVERWRITE sshd_config, restart sshd service, and create firewall rules on the Windows host." \
    "OVERWRITE"

  command -v powershell.exe >/dev/null 2>&1 \
    || buc_die "powershell.exe not found — this command must run from WSL"

  buc_step "Bootstrap: OpenSSH Server on Windows"

  # Step 1: Probe sshd
  buc_step "Probing OpenSSH Server..."
  local z_status=""
  if z_status=$(powershell.exe -Command 'Get-Service sshd -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Status' 2>&1); then
    z_status=$(printf '%s' "${z_status}" | tr -d '\r\n')
    buc_step "OpenSSH Server status: ${z_status}"
  else
    buc_step "OpenSSH Server not installed — installing..."
    powershell.exe -Command 'Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0' \
      || buc_die "Failed to install OpenSSH Server (elevated terminal required)"
    buc_step "OpenSSH Server installed"
  fi

  # Step 2: Write sshd_config from template
  buc_step "Writing sshd_config..."
  {
    echo "# Generated by buw-wcb — BURN bootstrap"
    echo "Port ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
    echo "PasswordAuthentication no"
    echo "PubkeyAuthentication yes"
    echo "PermitEmptyPasswords no"
    echo "AuthorizedKeysFile .ssh/authorized_keys"
    echo "Subsystem sftp sftp-server.exe"
    echo "Match Group administrators"
    echo "  AuthorizedKeysFile __PROGRAMDATA__/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys"
  } | powershell.exe -Command "\$input | Set-Content -Path '${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG}' -Force" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write sshd_config (elevated terminal required)"
  buc_step "sshd_config written"

  # Validate sshd_config
  powershell.exe -Command "sshd -t -f '${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG}'" \
    || buc_die "sshd_config validation failed — check config syntax"
  buc_step "sshd_config validated"

  # Step 3: Configure and start sshd service
  buc_step "Configuring sshd service..."
  powershell.exe -Command 'Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic' \
    || buc_die "Failed to configure sshd service"
  powershell.exe -Command 'Restart-Service sshd' \
    || buc_die "Failed to restart sshd service"
  buc_step "sshd service configured and started"

  # Step 4: Firewall rule (idempotent)
  buc_step "Checking firewall rule..."
  if powershell.exe -Command "Get-NetFirewallRule -Name '${ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME}' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_step "Firewall rule already exists"
  else
    powershell.exe -Command "New-NetFirewallRule -Name '${ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME}' -DisplayName '${ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME}' -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to create firewall rule"
    buc_step "Firewall rule created"
  fi

  # Step 5: Loopback verify
  buc_step "Verifying loopback SSH..."
  local z_whoami=""
  if z_whoami=$(ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no localhost whoami 2>&1); then
    z_whoami=$(printf '%s' "${z_whoami}" | tr -d '\r\n')
    buc_success "Bootstrap complete — loopback SSH: ${z_whoami}"
  else
    buc_warn "Loopback verify deferred (keys may need setup from client machine)"
    buc_step "Bootstrap structurally complete"
  fi
}

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUW Zipper - Colophon registry for BUK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUWZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUWZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zbuwz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buwz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled (CLI furnish must kindle buz first)
  zbuz_sentinel

  # TabTarget subsystem (buut_cli.sh)
  local z_mod="buut_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LIST_LAUNCHERS      "buw-tt-ll"  "${z_mod}" "buut_list_launchers"               ""  "List all registered launchers"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_LOGGING       "buw-tt-cbl" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_logging"      ""  "Create batch tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_NOLOG         "buw-tt-cbn" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_nolog"        ""  "Create batch tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_LOGGING "buw-tt-cil" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_logging" ""  "Create interactive tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_NOLOG   "buw-tt-cin" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_nolog"  ""  "Create interactive tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LAUNCHER            "buw-tt-cl"  "${z_mod}" "buut_launcher"                     ""  "Create launcher script"

  # Config Regime subsystem (burc_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burc_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE "buw-rcv" "${z_mod}" "burc_validate"  ""  "Validate BURC regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_RENDER   "buw-rcr" "${z_mod}" "burc_render"    ""  "Render BURC regime"

  # Station Regime subsystem (burs_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burs_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE "buw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "burs_validate"  ""  "Validate BURS regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_RENDER   "buw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "burs_render"    ""  "Render BURS regime"

  # Environment Regime subsystem (bure_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bure_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_VALIDATE "buw-rev" "${z_mod}" "bure_validate"  ""  "Validate BURE regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_RENDER   "buw-rer" "${z_mod}" "bure_render"    ""  "Render BURE regime"

  # Node Regime subsystem (burn_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burn_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_VALIDATE "buw-rnv" "${z_mod}" "burn_validate" "param1" "Validate BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_RENDER   "buw-rnr" "${z_mod}" "burn_render"   "param1" "Render BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_LIST     "buw-rnl" "${z_mod}" "burn_list"     ""       "List BURN profiles"

  # Qualification subsystem (buq_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buq_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_QUALIFY_SHELLCHECK "buw-qsc" "${z_mod}" "buq_shellcheck"  ""  "Run shellcheck on all tools"

  # Test fixtures (bux_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bux_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_DELAY "buw-xd" "${z_mod}" "bux_delay"  ""  "Sleep 20 seconds (timing fixture)"

  # Self-test (butt_testbench.sh)
  z_mod="butt_testbench.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_SELF_TEST "buw-st" "${z_mod}" "buw-st"  ""  "BUK self-test (kick-tires + bure-tweak)"

  # Node Regime — constructors + SSH automation (burn_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burn_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_LINUX      "buw-rhcl"  "${z_mod}" "burn_construct_linux"      ""        "Construct BURN profile: Linux"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_MAC        "buw-rhcm"  "${z_mod}" "burn_construct_mac"        ""        "Construct BURN profile: macOS"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_CYGWIN     "buw-rhcc"  "${z_mod}" "burn_construct_cygwin"     ""        "Construct BURN profile: Cygwin"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_WSL        "buw-rhcw"  "${z_mod}" "burn_construct_wsl"        ""        "Construct BURN profile: WSL"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_POWERSHELL "buw-rhcp"  "${z_mod}" "burn_construct_powershell" ""        "Construct BURN profile: PowerShell"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_LOCALHOST  "buw-rhcx"  "${z_mod}" "burn_construct_localhost"  ""        "Construct BURN profile: localhost"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_VERIFY_SSH  "buw-HWvs"  "${z_mod}" "burn_verify_ssh"           "param1"  "Verify SSH to BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_INSTALL_KEY "buw-rnk"   "${z_mod}" "burn_install_key"          "param1"  "Install BURN key into authorized_keys"

  # Windows Commands (buwc_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buwc_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_WC_BOOTSTRAP   "buw-wcb"   "${z_mod}" "buwc_bootstrap_sshd"       ""        "Provision sshd on Windows (WSL)"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_WC_INSTALL_KEY "buw-wck"   "${z_mod}" "buwc_install_key"          "param1"  "Install BURN key into Windows admin authorized_keys"

  # Handbook — top index + Windows OS procedures (buhw_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buhw_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_H0_TOP            "buw-h0"    "${z_mod}" "buhw_handbook_top"        ""        "BUK top-level handbook index"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_TOP            "buw-hw"    "${z_mod}" "buhw_top"                ""        "BUK-level Windows procedures checklist"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_BASE    "buw-HWab"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_base"        ""        "OpenSSH server install + lockdown"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_REMOTE  "buw-HWar"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_remote"      ""        "Client key generation"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_ENTRY   "buw-HWax"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_entrypoints" ""        "SSH command= routing + icacls"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ENV_WSL        "buw-HWew"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_environment_wsl"    "param1"  "WSL distro creation"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ENV_CYGWIN     "buw-HWec"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_environment_cygwin" ""        "Cygwin install"

  readonly ZBUWZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)

zbuwz_healthcheck() {
  zbuwz_sentinel
  buz_healthcheck
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zbuwz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buwz not kindled - call zbuwz_kindle first"
}

38	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burn_regime.sh"
39	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buhw_windows.sh"
40	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buwc_windows.sh"

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUK Windows Commands - Command Line Interface
#
# Furnish: needs buhw tinder (Windows constants) and buc_require.
# No regime stack — commands consume constants directly.

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

zbuwc_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burn_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buhw_windows.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buwc_windows.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce

  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"

  zburc_kindle
  zburc_enforce

  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"

  zburs_kindle
  zburs_enforce

  zbuhw_kindle
  zbuwc_kindle

  # If BUZ_FOLIO is set, load and kindle the specified profile
  if test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
    local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burn.env"
    test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "BURN profile not found: ${z_profile_file}"
    source "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURN: ${z_profile_file}"
    zburn_kindle
    zburn_enforce
  fi
}

buc_execute buwc_ "Windows Commands" zbuwc_furnish "$@"

# eof

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BURN Regime - Bash Utility Regime Node Module
20	#
21	# BURN is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
22	# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burn.env
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	# Multiple inclusion detection
27	test -z "${ZBURN_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
28	ZBURN_SOURCED=1
29	
30	######################################################################
31	# Internal Functions (zburn_*)
32	
33	zburn_kindle() {
34	  test -z "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn already kindled"
35	
36	  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
37	  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
38	
39	  # Enroll all BURN variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering
40	
41	  buv_regime_enroll BURN
42	
43	  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
44	  buv_string_enroll  BURN_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
45	  buv_xname_enroll   BURN_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
46	  buv_xname_enroll   BURN_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"
47	
48	  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
49	  buv_string_enroll  BURN_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"
50	  buv_string_enroll  BURN_KEY_FILE     0  256  "SSH private key filename in ~/.ssh/ (empty defaults to alias)"
51	
52	  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
53	  buv_string_enroll  BURN_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"
54	
55	  buv_group_enroll "Privilege Tier"
56	  buv_enum_enroll    BURN_TIER  "Tier classification: privileged for node-level ops, workload for ephemeral remote work"  privileged  workload
57	
58	  # Guard against unexpected BURN_ variables not in enrollment
59	  buv_scope_sentinel BURN BURN_
60	
61	  # Lock all enrolled BURN_ variables against mutation
62	  buv_lock BURN
63	
64	  readonly ZBURN_KINDLED=1
65	}
66	
67	zburn_sentinel() {
68	  test "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burn not kindled - call zburn_kindle first"
69	}
70	
71	# Enforce all BURN enrollment validations
72	zburn_enforce() {
73	  zburn_sentinel
74	
75	  buv_vet BURN
76	}
77	
78	######################################################################
79	# Public Functions (burn_*)
80	
81	# Build a BURN authorized_keys line with alias marker.
82	# Pure string builder — no I/O. Caller decides how to write.
83	# Sets ZBURN_KEY_LINE (the full line) and ZBURN_KEY_MARKER (the grep tag).
84	# Prerequisite: BURN kindled (needs BURN_ALIAS, BURN_COMMAND, BURN_SSH_PUBKEY)
85	zburn_build_key_line() {
86	  zburn_sentinel
87	
88	  ZBURN_KEY_MARKER="# BURN:${BURN_ALIAS}"
89	
90	  if test -n "${BURN_COMMAND}"; then
91	    ZBURN_KEY_LINE="command=\"${BURN_COMMAND}\" ${BURN_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}"
92	  else
93	    ZBURN_KEY_LINE="${BURN_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}"
94	  fi
95	}
96	
97	# List available alias names as space-separated tokens.
98	# Emits one <alias>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS} observation fact per profile (empty
99	# content; presence is the fact).
100	# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
101	burn_list_capture() {
102	  zburs_sentinel
103	
104	  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
105	  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1
106	
107	  local z_result=""
108	  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burn.env)
109	  local z_i=""
110	  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
111	    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
112	    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
113	    local z_alias="${z_dir##*/}"
114	    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_alias}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
115	    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_alias}"
116	  done
117	  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
118	  echo "${z_result}"
119	}
120	
121	# Assert that the named alias's profile has the expected BURN_TIER value.
122	# Exits non-zero with diagnostic before any side effect on tier mismatch.
123	# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (BURS_USER), BURD kindled (BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
124	burn_assert_tier() {
125	  zburs_sentinel
126	
127	  local -r z_expected="${1:-}"
128	  local -r z_alias="${2:-}"
129	  test -n "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: expected tier required (privileged|workload)"
130	  test -n "${z_alias}"    || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias required"
131	
132	  local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}/burn.env"
133	  test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: profile not found for alias '${z_alias}': ${z_profile_file}"
134	
135	  local z_observed=""
136	  local z_line=""
137	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
138	    case "${z_line}" in
139	      BURN_TIER=*) z_observed="${z_line#BURN_TIER=}" ; break ;;
140	    esac
141	  done < "${z_profile_file}"
142	
143	  test -n "${z_observed}"                || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: BURN_TIER missing or empty in profile for alias '${z_alias}'"
144	  test "${z_observed}" = "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias '${z_alias}' has BURN_TIER='${z_observed}', expected '${z_expected}'"
145	}
146	
147	# eof
148

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURN Regime - Bash Utility Regime Node Module
#
# BURN is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burn.env

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURN_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURN_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburn_*)

zburn_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all BURN variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURN

  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURN_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURN_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"

  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_KEY_FILE     0  256  "SSH private key filename in ~/.ssh/ (empty defaults to alias)"

  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"

  buv_group_enroll "Privilege Tier"
  buv_enum_enroll    BURN_TIER  "Tier classification: privileged for node-level ops, workload for ephemeral remote work"  privileged  workload

  # Guard against unexpected BURN_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURN BURN_

  # Lock all enrolled BURN_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURN

  readonly ZBURN_KINDLED=1
}

zburn_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burn not kindled - call zburn_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURN enrollment validations
zburn_enforce() {
  zburn_sentinel

  buv_vet BURN
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (burn_*)

# Build a BURN authorized_keys line with alias marker.
# Pure string builder — no I/O. Caller decides how to write.
# Sets ZBURN_KEY_LINE (the full line) and ZBURN_KEY_MARKER (the grep tag).
# Prerequisite: BURN kindled (needs BURN_ALIAS, BURN_COMMAND, BURN_SSH_PUBKEY)
zburn_build_key_line() {
  zburn_sentinel

  ZBURN_KEY_MARKER="# BURN:${BURN_ALIAS}"

  if test -n "${BURN_COMMAND}"; then
    ZBURN_KEY_LINE="command=\"${BURN_COMMAND}\" ${BURN_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}"
  else
    ZBURN_KEY_LINE="${BURN_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}"
  fi
}

# List available alias names as space-separated tokens.
# Emits one <alias>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS} observation fact per profile (empty
# content; presence is the fact).
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burn_list_capture() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burn.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    local z_alias="${z_dir##*/}"
    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_alias}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_alias}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

# Assert that the named alias's profile has the expected BURN_TIER value.
# Exits non-zero with diagnostic before any side effect on tier mismatch.
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (BURS_USER), BURD kindled (BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burn_assert_tier() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_expected="${1:-}"
  local -r z_alias="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: expected tier required (privileged|workload)"
  test -n "${z_alias}"    || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias required"

  local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}/burn.env"
  test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: profile not found for alias '${z_alias}': ${z_profile_file}"

  local z_observed=""
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    case "${z_line}" in
      BURN_TIER=*) z_observed="${z_line#BURN_TIER=}" ; break ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_profile_file}"

  test -n "${z_observed}"                || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: BURN_TIER missing or empty in profile for alias '${z_alias}'"
  test "${z_observed}" = "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias '${z_alias}' has BURN_TIER='${z_observed}', expected '${z_expected}'"
}

# eof

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BURN CLI - Command line interface for BURN node regime operations
20	#
21	# Four command families:
22	#   burn_validate/render/list  — regime read operations (require BUZ_FOLIO)
23	#   burn_construct_*           — profile constructors (write burn.env)
24	#   burn_verify_*              — SSH automation (verify)
25	#   burn_install_key           — authorized_keys management (per-profile, idempotent)
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
30	
31	######################################################################
32	# Command Functions — Regime Operations
33	
34	burn_validate() {
35	  buc_doc_brief "Validate BURN node regime profile via enrollment report"
36	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
37	
38	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
39	  buc_step "Validating BURN node regime"
40	  buv_report BURN "Node Regime"
41	  buc_step "BURN node regime valid"
42	}
43	
44	burn_render() {
45	  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of BURN node regime profile"
46	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
47	
48	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
49	  buv_render BURN "BURN - Bash Utility Node Regime"
50	}
51	
52	burn_list() {
53	  buc_doc_brief "List available BURN host profiles for current user"
54	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
55	
56	  local z_aliases
57	  z_aliases=$(burn_list_capture) || buc_die "No BURN profiles found for user: ${BURS_USER}"
58	  buc_step "Available profiles for ${BURS_USER}:"
59	  local z_alias=""
60	  for z_alias in ${z_aliases}; do
61	    buc_bare "        ${z_alias}"
62	  done
63	}
64	
65	######################################################################
66	# Internal: Constructor Helper
67	#
68	# Shared logic for all platform constructors.
69	# Args: host user moniker suffix command_value key_file
70	# command_value and key_file may be empty.
71	
72	zburn_construct() {
73	  local z_host="${1:-}"
74	  local z_user="${2:-}"
75	  local z_moniker="${3:-}"
76	  local z_suffix="${4:-}"
77	  local z_command="${5:-}"
78	  local z_key_file="${6:-}"
79	
80	  test -n "${z_host}"    || buc_die "host required (arg 1)"
81	  test -n "${z_user}"    || buc_die "user required (arg 2)"
82	  test -n "${z_moniker}" || buc_die "moniker required (arg 3)"
83	
84	  local -r z_alias="${z_moniker}-${z_suffix}"
85	  local -r z_profile_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}"
86	  local -r z_profile_file="${z_profile_dir}/burn.env"
87	  local -r z_pubkey_file="${HOME}/.ssh/${z_alias}.pub"
88	  local z_pubkey=""
89	  local z_has_pubkey=0
90	
91	  if test -f "${z_pubkey_file}"; then
92	    z_pubkey=$(head -1 "${z_pubkey_file}")
93	    z_has_pubkey=1
94	    buc_step "Found public key: ${z_pubkey_file}"
95	  else
96	    z_pubkey="ssh-ed25519 REPLACE_WITH_ACTUAL_PUBKEY ${BURS_USER}@$(hostname -s)"
97	  fi
98	
99	  mkdir -p "${z_profile_dir}"
100	
101	  printf '%s\n' "BURN_HOST=${z_host}"           >  "${z_profile_file}"
102	  printf '%s\n' "BURN_USER=${z_user}"            >> "${z_profile_file}"
103	  printf '%s\n' "BURN_ALIAS=${z_alias}"          >> "${z_profile_file}"
104	  printf '%s\n' "BURN_SSH_PUBKEY='${z_pubkey}'"  >> "${z_profile_file}"
105	  printf '%s\n' "BURN_KEY_FILE=${z_key_file}"    >> "${z_profile_file}"
106	  printf '%s\n' "BURN_COMMAND='${z_command}'"    >> "${z_profile_file}"
107	
108	  buc_step "BURN profile written: ${z_profile_file}"
109	
110	  if test "${z_has_pubkey}" = "0"; then
111	    buh_e
112	    buh_section "SSH Key Required"
113	    buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_pubkey_file}"; local -r z_pubkey_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
114	    buh_line "No public key at ${z_pubkey_yelp}."
115	    buh_line   "Generate one:"
116	    buh_e
117	    buh_code   "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_alias}"
118	    buh_e
119	    buh_line   "Then re-run this constructor to populate the public key."
120	  fi
121	}
122	
123	######################################################################
124	# Command Functions — Constructors
125	
126	burn_construct_linux() {
127	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for Linux target"
128	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Linux machine"
129	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
130	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-linux)"
131	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
132	
133	  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "linux" ""
134	}
135	
136	burn_construct_mac() {
137	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for macOS target"
138	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Mac"
139	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
140	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-mac)"
141	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
142	
143	  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "mac" ""
144	}
145	
146	burn_construct_cygwin() {
147	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for Windows Cygwin target"
148	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
149	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
150	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-cyg)"
151	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
152	
153	  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "cyg" 'C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l'
154	}
155	
156	burn_construct_wsl() {
157	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for Windows WSL target"
158	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
159	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
160	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-wsl)"
161	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
162	
163	  local -r z_wsl_distro="Ubuntu"
164	  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "wsl" "C:\\Windows\\System32\\wsl.exe -d ${z_wsl_distro}"
165	}
166	
167	burn_construct_powershell() {
168	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for Windows PowerShell target"
169	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
170	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
171	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-ps)"
172	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
173	
174	  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "ps" 'C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe'
175	}
176	
177	burn_construct_localhost() {
178	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for localhost (local SSH)"
179	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on this machine"
180	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-local)"
181	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
182	
183	  zburn_construct "localhost" "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "local" "" "id_ed25519"
184	}
185	
186	######################################################################
187	# Command Functions — SSH Automation
188	
189	burn_verify_ssh() {
190	  buc_doc_brief "Verify SSH connectivity to a BURN profile"
191	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
192	
193	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
194	
195	  buc_step "Testing SSH to ${BURN_ALIAS} (${BURN_HOST})..."
196	  local z_result=""
197	  if z_result=$(ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=10 "${BURN_ALIAS}" whoami 2>&1); then
198	    buc_success "SSH OK: ${BURN_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
199	  else
200	    buc_die "SSH FAILED: ${BURN_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
201	  fi
202	}
203	
204	burn_install_key() {
205	  buc_doc_brief "Install BURN profile SSH key into authorized_keys (idempotent)"
206	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
207	
208	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
209	
210	  local -r z_auth_keys="${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys"
211	
212	  zburn_build_key_line
213	
214	  mkdir -p "${HOME}/.ssh"
215	
216	  if test -f "${z_auth_keys}"; then
217	    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
218	    grep -v "${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}" "${z_auth_keys}" > "${z_temp}" || true
219	    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_auth_keys}"
220	  fi
221	
222	  printf '%s\n' "${ZBURN_KEY_LINE}" >> "${z_auth_keys}"
223	  chmod 600 "${z_auth_keys}"
224	
225	  buc_success "Key installed for ${BURN_ALIAS} in ${z_auth_keys}"
226	}
227	
228	######################################################################
229	# Furnish and Main
230	
231	zburn_furnish() {
232	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
233	  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
234	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
235	
236	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
237	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
238	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
239	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
240	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
241	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burn_regime.sh"
242	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
243	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
244	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"
245	
246	  zbuv_kindle
247	  zburd_kindle
248	  zburd_enforce
249	
250	  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"
251	
252	  zburc_kindle
253	  zburc_enforce
254	
255	  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"
256	
257	  zburs_kindle
258	  zburs_enforce
259	
260	  zbupr_kindle
261	
262	  # If BUZ_FOLIO is set, load and kindle the specified profile
263	  if test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
264	    local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burn.env"
265	    test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "BURN profile not found: ${z_profile_file}"
266	    source "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURN: ${z_profile_file}"
267	    zburn_kindle
268	    zburn_enforce
269	  fi
270	}
271	
272	buc_execute burn_ "Bash Utility Node Regime" zburn_furnish "$@"
273	
274	# eof
275

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURN CLI - Command line interface for BURN node regime operations
#
# Four command families:
#   burn_validate/render/list  — regime read operations (require BUZ_FOLIO)
#   burn_construct_*           — profile constructors (write burn.env)
#   burn_verify_*              — SSH automation (verify)
#   burn_install_key           — authorized_keys management (per-profile, idempotent)

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command Functions — Regime Operations

burn_validate() {
  buc_doc_brief "Validate BURN node regime profile via enrollment report"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
  buc_step "Validating BURN node regime"
  buv_report BURN "Node Regime"
  buc_step "BURN node regime valid"
}

burn_render() {
  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of BURN node regime profile"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
  buv_render BURN "BURN - Bash Utility Node Regime"
}

burn_list() {
  buc_doc_brief "List available BURN host profiles for current user"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_aliases
  z_aliases=$(burn_list_capture) || buc_die "No BURN profiles found for user: ${BURS_USER}"
  buc_step "Available profiles for ${BURS_USER}:"
  local z_alias=""
  for z_alias in ${z_aliases}; do
    buc_bare "        ${z_alias}"
  done
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Constructor Helper
#
# Shared logic for all platform constructors.
# Args: host user moniker suffix command_value key_file
# command_value and key_file may be empty.

zburn_construct() {
  local z_host="${1:-}"
  local z_user="${2:-}"
  local z_moniker="${3:-}"
  local z_suffix="${4:-}"
  local z_command="${5:-}"
  local z_key_file="${6:-}"

  test -n "${z_host}"    || buc_die "host required (arg 1)"
  test -n "${z_user}"    || buc_die "user required (arg 2)"
  test -n "${z_moniker}" || buc_die "moniker required (arg 3)"

  local -r z_alias="${z_moniker}-${z_suffix}"
  local -r z_profile_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}"
  local -r z_profile_file="${z_profile_dir}/burn.env"
  local -r z_pubkey_file="${HOME}/.ssh/${z_alias}.pub"
  local z_pubkey=""
  local z_has_pubkey=0

  if test -f "${z_pubkey_file}"; then
    z_pubkey=$(head -1 "${z_pubkey_file}")
    z_has_pubkey=1
    buc_step "Found public key: ${z_pubkey_file}"
  else
    z_pubkey="ssh-ed25519 REPLACE_WITH_ACTUAL_PUBKEY ${BURS_USER}@$(hostname -s)"
  fi

  mkdir -p "${z_profile_dir}"

  printf '%s\n' "BURN_HOST=${z_host}"           >  "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURN_USER=${z_user}"            >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURN_ALIAS=${z_alias}"          >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURN_SSH_PUBKEY='${z_pubkey}'"  >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURN_KEY_FILE=${z_key_file}"    >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURN_COMMAND='${z_command}'"    >> "${z_profile_file}"

  buc_step "BURN profile written: ${z_profile_file}"

  if test "${z_has_pubkey}" = "0"; then
    buh_e
    buh_section "SSH Key Required"
    buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_pubkey_file}"; local -r z_pubkey_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
    buh_line "No public key at ${z_pubkey_yelp}."
    buh_line   "Generate one:"
    buh_e
    buh_code   "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_alias}"
    buh_e
    buh_line   "Then re-run this constructor to populate the public key."
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Command Functions — Constructors

burn_construct_linux() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for Linux target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Linux machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-linux)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "linux" ""
}

burn_construct_mac() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for macOS target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Mac"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-mac)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "mac" ""
}

burn_construct_cygwin() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for Windows Cygwin target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-cyg)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "cyg" 'C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l'
}

burn_construct_wsl() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for Windows WSL target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-wsl)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_wsl_distro="Ubuntu"
  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "wsl" "C:\\Windows\\System32\\wsl.exe -d ${z_wsl_distro}"
}

burn_construct_powershell() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for Windows PowerShell target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-ps)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "ps" 'C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe'
}

burn_construct_localhost() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for localhost (local SSH)"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on this machine"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-local)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburn_construct "localhost" "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "local" "" "id_ed25519"
}

######################################################################
# Command Functions — SSH Automation

burn_verify_ssh() {
  buc_doc_brief "Verify SSH connectivity to a BURN profile"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"

  buc_step "Testing SSH to ${BURN_ALIAS} (${BURN_HOST})..."
  local z_result=""
  if z_result=$(ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=10 "${BURN_ALIAS}" whoami 2>&1); then
    buc_success "SSH OK: ${BURN_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
  else
    buc_die "SSH FAILED: ${BURN_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
  fi
}

burn_install_key() {
  buc_doc_brief "Install BURN profile SSH key into authorized_keys (idempotent)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"

  local -r z_auth_keys="${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys"

  zburn_build_key_line

  mkdir -p "${HOME}/.ssh"

  if test -f "${z_auth_keys}"; then
    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
    grep -v "${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}" "${z_auth_keys}" > "${z_temp}" || true
    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_auth_keys}"
  fi

  printf '%s\n' "${ZBURN_KEY_LINE}" >> "${z_auth_keys}"
  chmod 600 "${z_auth_keys}"

  buc_success "Key installed for ${BURN_ALIAS} in ${z_auth_keys}"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zburn_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burn_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce

  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"

  zburc_kindle
  zburc_enforce

  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"

  zburs_kindle
  zburs_enforce

  zbupr_kindle

  # If BUZ_FOLIO is set, load and kindle the specified profile
  if test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
    local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burn.env"
    test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "BURN profile not found: ${z_profile_file}"
    source "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURN: ${z_profile_file}"
    zburn_kindle
    zburn_enforce
  fi
}

buc_execute burn_ "Bash Utility Node Regime" zburn_furnish "$@"

# eof

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Bash Utility Handbook - Windows OS Procedures
20	#
21	# Generic Windows mechanisms for SSH access, WSL, and Cygwin setup.
22	# All output is handbook display (buh_* combinators only).
23	# Project-specific topology and Docker policy live in rbhw_windows.sh.
24	
25	set -euo pipefail
26	
27	# Multiple inclusion detection
28	test -z "${ZBUHW_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhw multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
29	ZBUHW_SOURCED=1
30	
31	######################################################################
32	# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel
33	
34	zbuhw_kindle() {
35	  test -z "${ZBUHW_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhw already kindled"
36	
37	  # Tinder — fixed Windows paths and network constants
38	  # Forward slashes throughout — works in PowerShell, display, and icacls
39	  readonly ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG='C:/ProgramData/ssh/sshd_config'
40	  readonly ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS='C:/ProgramData/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys'
41	  readonly ZBUHW_CYGWIN_ROOT='C:/cygwin64'
42	  readonly ZBUHW_CYGWIN_BASH='C:/cygwin64/bin/bash.exe'
43	  readonly ZBUHW_SSH_PORT="22"
44	  readonly ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME="sshd"
45	  readonly ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME="OpenSSH Server"
46	
47	  readonly ZBUHW_KINDLED=1
48	}
49	
50	zbuhw_sentinel() {
51	  test "${ZBUHW_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buhw not kindled - call zbuhw_kindle first"
52	}
53	
54	######################################################################
55	# External Functions (buhw_*)
56	
57	buhw_access_base() {
58	  zbuhw_sentinel
59	
60	  buc_doc_brief "Display OpenSSH server installation and lockdown procedure"
61	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
62	
63	  buh_section  "OpenSSH Server Installation & Lockdown"
64	  buh_line     "Establish Windows as a keys-only SSH endpoint."
65	  buh_e
66	  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
67	  buh_line     "- Windows host with administrator access"
68	  buh_line     "- Network reachable on TCP/${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
69	  buh_e
70	  buh_step1    "Open Elevated PowerShell:"
71	  buh_line     "Right-click Start → Terminal (Admin), or search 'PowerShell' and Run as Administrator."
72	  buh_line     "All commands below run in this elevated session."
73	  buh_e
74	  buh_step1    "Install OpenSSH Server:"
75	  buh_line     "This downloads from Windows Update — may take 10+ minutes."
76	  buh_code     "Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0"
77	  buh_e
78	  buh_step1    "Enable OpenSSH Server:"
79	  buh_code     "Start-Service sshd"
80	  buh_code     "Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic"
81	  buh_e
82	  buh_step1    "Allow Port ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT} Through Firewall:"
83	  buh_code     "New-NetFirewallRule -Name ${ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME} -DisplayName \"${ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME}\" -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
84	  buh_e
85	  buh_step1    "Configure sshd_config:"
86	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG}"; local -r z_sshd_config_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
87	  buh_line     "File: ${z_sshd_config_yelp}"
88	  buh_line     "This file is SYSTEM-owned. Edit a temp copy, then replace."
89	  buh_e
90	  buh_step2    "Copy to editable location:"
91	  buh_code     "Copy-Item ${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG} \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
92	  buh_e
93	  buh_step2    "Edit the copy:"
94	  buh_code     "notepad \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
95	  buh_line     "Set these directives (add if not present):"
96	  buh_code     "PasswordAuthentication no"
97	  buh_code     "PubkeyAuthentication yes"
98	  buh_code     "PermitEmptyPasswords no"
99	  buh_line     "Do NOT add UsePAM or ChallengeResponseAuthentication — Windows OpenSSH"
100	  buh_line     "rejects unrecognized directives and the service will fail to start."
101	  buh_e
102	  buh_step2    "Validate before applying:"
103	  buh_code     "sshd -t -f \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
104	  buh_line     "Expect: no output (silence means valid). Fix any reported errors."
105	  buh_e
106	  buh_step2    "Replace the original:"
107	  buh_code     "Copy-Item \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config ${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG} -Force"
108	  buh_e
109	  buh_step1    "Restart Service:"
110	  buh_code     "Restart-Service sshd"
111	  buh_e
112	  buh_step1    "Verify:"
113	  buh_line     "Discover your Windows username and IP (on the Windows machine):"
114	  buh_code     "whoami"
115	  buh_code     "ipconfig"
116	  buh_line     "Note the username (after the backslash) and IPv4 address."
117	  buh_e
118	  buh_line     "From a remote machine, test SSH reachability:"
119	  buh_code     "ssh <username>@<ip>"
120	  buh_line     "Expect: Permission denied (publickey). This confirms sshd is running"
121	  buh_line     "and password login is correctly rejected. Key setup follows in step 2."
122	
123	}
124	
125	buhw_access_remote() {
126	  zbuhw_sentinel
127	
128	  buc_doc_brief "Display SSH client key generation procedure (args: host user key-name alias)"
129	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
130	
131	  local z_host="${1:-}"
132	  local z_user="${2:-}"
133	  local z_key_name="${3:-}"
134	  local z_alias="${4:-}"
135	
136	  test -n "${z_host}"     || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: host required (arg 1)"
137	  test -n "${z_user}"     || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: user required (arg 2)"
138	  test -n "${z_key_name}" || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: key-name required (arg 3)"
139	  test -n "${z_alias}"    || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: alias required (arg 4)"
140	
141	  buh_section  "SSH Client Key Generation"
142	  buh_line     "Provision client with a dedicated key for the target host."
143	  buh_e
144	  buh_section  "Parameters:"
145	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_host}"; local -r z_host_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
146	  buh_line     "  Host:     ${z_host_yelp}"
147	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_user}"; local -r z_user_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
148	  buh_line     "  User:     ${z_user_yelp}"
149	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_key_name}"; local -r z_key_name_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
150	  buh_line     "  Key name: ${z_key_name_yelp}"
151	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_alias}"; local -r z_alias_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
152	  buh_line     "  Alias:    ${z_alias_yelp}"
153	  buh_e
154	  buh_step1    "Generate Key:"
155	  buh_code     "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}"
156	  buh_e
157	  buh_step1    "Copy Public Key to Host:"
158	  buh_line     "The public key must be added to the host's authorized_keys."
159	  buyy_cmd_yawp "cat ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}.pub"; local -r z_pubkey_cmd_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
160	  buh_line     "Display the key: ${z_pubkey_cmd_yelp}"
161	  buh_line     "Copy the output for use in the entrypoints procedure."
162	  buh_e
163	  buh_section  "Verification:"
164	  buh_tt       ""  "${BUWZ_HW_VERIFY_SSH}"  ""  " ${z_alias}"
165	  buh_line     "Expect: connects (may not yet enter target env — routing not configured)."

194	  buyy_ui_yawp "AAAA.../BBBB.../CCCC..."; local -r z_key_placeholders_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
